texto: Often, we find ourselves wedged in the middle of a draining conversation. We might desperately want to dislodge ourselves from the interaction, but instead we stay in receiving mode, absorbing their words like a slow-acting poison.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michaela Chung: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a autoestima. O trecho “Often, we find ourselves wedged in the middle of a draining conversation. We might desperately wa…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, confidence, introversion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e introversion.

frase_original: Often, we find ourselves wedged in the middle of a draining conversation. We might desperately want to dislodge ourselves from the interaction, but instead we stay in receiving mode, absorbing their words like a slow-acting poison.

texto: We don't need to be in a designated role to take ownership of our environment. We can simply decide that we will be the giver, the helper, or the greeter wherever we are. We can even make a little game of it. We can tell ourselves: Today, I'm going to make at least three new people feel welcome. Or we can say: At this party, I'm going to gift two people a genuine compliment.
explicacao: Citação de Michaela Chung sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “We don't need to be in a designated role to take ownership of our environment. We can simply deci…” articula autoestima, confidence, introversion em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como introversion.

frase_original: We don't need to be in a designated role to take ownership of our environment. We can simply decide that we will be the giver, the helper, or the greeter wherever we are. We can even make a little game of it. We can tell ourselves: Today, I'm going to make at least three new people feel welcome. Or we can say: At this party, I'm going to gift two people a genuine compliment.
